name: TaskClaw Default Boards
description: Default seed boards for new TaskClaw accounts. Includes the X Content Pipeline and Personal Board with all required categories, skills, and agent configurations.
author: system@taskclaw.co

_readme.how_slugs_work: Every entity uses a deterministic slug for cross-referencing. When imported, the system creates UUIDs but maintains slug-based lookups so categories, skills, and board steps stay connected.
_readme.import_order: 1) Categories → 2) Skills → 3) category_skills junction → 4) Board template → 5) Board instance + steps with linked_category_id resolved from slugs

entity_relationships.board.default_category_slug: References a category by slug. Resolved to default_category_id on import.
entity_relationships.step.linked_category_slug: References a category by slug. Resolved to linked_category_id on import.
entity_relationships.category.skills[].slug: Skills embedded in the category. Created and linked via category_skills junction.
entity_relationships.category.knowledge_docs[]: Knowledge docs embedded in the category. Created with category_id FK.

slug_conventions.boards: kebab-case, globally unique per account (e.g., x-content-pipeline)
slug_conventions.categories: kebab-case, unique per account (e.g., x-copywriter, personal-secretary)
slug_conventions.skills: kebab-case, unique per account (e.g., x-post-writer, personal-task-planner)
slug_conventions.steps: kebab-case, unique per board (e.g., idea, drafting, inbox, planning)

_readme.extending: To add a new seed board: create a new JSON file following the manifest_version 1.0 schema, add it to the boards array, and run the seed migration.
